title: Agents
subtitle: Customize your predefined agent presets with a dedicated workspace for metadata, prompts, tools, and feature toggles.

generic: Give me a memory to hold.
I can help sort this out.
Let us catch the important bits.
I will recall useful context when it helps.
Let us organize the messy part.
I am here for the next step.
Hi. I saved you a seat.
team: I can ask the other agents too.
We have a few minds ready.

default: I can use {feature} when it helps.
{feature} is ready if we need it.
I will bring in {feature} at the right moment.
knowledgeRetrieval: I will recall memory to answer you better.
I can look back at relevant context for you.
I will pull in useful memory when it helps.
citations: I can show where an answer came from.
I will keep sources close when they matter.
I can ground the answer with references.
multiAgent: I can ask another agent for focused help.
I will bring in the right agent if needed.
I can split the work with another agent.
web: I can check the web when fresh context matters.
I can open pages and read what changed.
I will look things up when current details matter.
artifact: I can turn the answer into a clearer artifact.
I can render structured work when it helps.
I can make the output easier to inspect.
documents: I can read and work with your documents.
I will use document context when it helps.
I can help draft, edit, or summarize files.
documentsFs: I can organize document files for you.
I can save useful work into the document library.
I will keep document outputs easy to find.
fileSystem: I can work with files when the task needs it.
I can read and update local project files.
I will keep file changes focused.
dailyBriefing: I can prepare a focused daily briefing.
I will pull the important updates together.
I can turn scattered updates into a morning brief.
financeTracker: I can research markets and keep the numbers clear.
I will compare financial details before answering.
I can turn finance research into a readable report.
jobApplication: I can tailor job application materials.
I will help align your resume with the role.
I can make application details sharper.
languageTutor: I can practice language with you step by step.
I will adapt explanations to your level.
I can turn mistakes into quick lessons.
mcp: I can use connected MCP tools when they help.
I will reach external tools through MCP if needed.
I can bring connected services into the answer.
mealPlanner: I can plan meals around your constraints.
I will balance recipes, timing, and preferences.
I can turn food ideas into a practical plan.
newsCollection: I can gather recent news and summarize it.
I will read beyond headlines when news matters.
I can collect updates into a concise brief.
nodejsSandbox: I can run small code checks in a sandbox.
I will test code ideas before trusting them.
I can use a Node.js sandbox for quick verification.
planner: I can break this into a practical plan.
I will keep the next steps clear.
I can turn a messy goal into ordered work.
shoppingAssistant: I can compare products before you choose.
I will check specs, prices, and tradeoffs.
I can make shopping research easier to scan.
travelPlanner: I can shape this into a useful itinerary.
I will balance places, timing, and logistics.
I can plan travel details with current context.
knowledgeGrow: I can turn details into lasting knowledge.
I will connect facts so they are easier to recall.
I can grow the memory graph from useful context.

morning: Good morning. Ready when you are.
Good morning. Your notes are ready.
afternoon: Good afternoon. Let us pick this up.
evening: Good evening. I am still here.
Evening. Let us make this easy.
Good evening. Want a quick recap?

list.title: Agent presets
list.subtitle: Browse and open a preset to inspect or customize its behavior.
list.searchPlaceholder: Search by preset name or description
list.caption: Preset library
list.emptyDescription: Create your first preset to start customizing a dedicated agent.

overview.subtitle: Edit the preset identity here, then refine the full execution config in the panel on the right.
overview.emptyDescription: Choose a preset from the directory to review its metadata and configuration summary.
overview.metadataDescription: These fields describe and organize the preset without changing the underlying workflow structure.

fields.name: Preset name
fields.namePlaceholder: Give this preset a clear, scannable name
fields.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the role, audience, or operating constraints for this preset
fields.iconScreenHint: Click a style, then pick text, color, or an emoji.

summary.promptsDescription: Prompt lengths help you spot overrides quickly without reading the full text.

config.title: Preset configuration
config.subtitle: Adjust prompts, graph type, feature toggles, and tool availability.

createDialog.title: Create agent preset
createDialog.description: Name the new preset. It will start from the default predefined configuration so you can customize it immediately.
createDialog.aiDescription: Describe the agent you want and let AI draft the name, instructions, tools, skills, and memory settings.

delete.title: Delete agent preset
delete.description: Delete "{{name}}"? This removes the preset and its saved configuration.
delete.linkedMemoryDescription: This agent is linked to the "{{name}}" memory topic. Leave this checked to delete that memory with the agent.
delete.linkedMemoryDeleteFailed: Failed to delete the linked memory topic. The agent was not deleted.
delete.lastPresetHint: At least one preset must remain available.

reset.title: Reset preset configuration
reset.description: This resets prompts, graph selection, features, and tools back to the default configuration for this preset.

confirm.discardSelection: You have unsaved changes. Discard them and switch presets?
confirm.leavePage: You have unsaved changes on this agent preset. Leave this page and discard them?

skills.label: Skills
skills.defaultHint: Published defaults are read-only, sourced from public skill repositories, filtered to avoid Python-backed skills, and loaded lazily from their upstream SKILL.md files.
skills.searchPlaceholder: Search skills by name, description, or source
skills.emptyDefault: No default skills matched your search.
skills.emptyFilteredCustom: No custom skills matched your search.

editor.nameHint: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Used as file name.

import.title: Import from GitHub
import.urlHint: Paste a GitHub file URL or raw.githubusercontent.com URL pointing to a .md skill file.

editor.bridgeHint: Browser MCP access supports HTTP or SSE. For local stdio MCP servers, copy a predefined supergateway command, run it in a terminal, then add the exposed local URL here.
editor.addDescription: Add an HTTP or SSE MCP endpoint exposed by a remote server or local supergateway bridge.

instructions.label: Instructions
instructions.placeholder: Describe your agent's role and behavior…

templatePanel.title: Choose how to build
templatePanel.subtitle: Build from conversation or start with a prepared agent shape.
templatePanel.blankTitle: No template, build completely with your messages
templatePanel.blankSubtitle: Start with an empty draft and let chat shape the agent.
templatePanel.blankDescription: Use chat to define the name, behavior, skills, tools, and constraints from an empty draft.
templatePanel.templatesSubtitle: Pick a starter and continue refining it in chat.

chatPanel.title: Build agent with AI
chatPanel.subtitle: Describe behavior, tools, skills, and constraints.
chatPanel.inputPlaceholder: Ask AI to build or update this agent...
chatPanel.inputPlaceholderNoModel: Select or load a model before chatting...

wizard.optimizeInitialMessage: Tell me how you want to improve this agent. I already loaded the current agent configuration, so I can update its name, description, instructions, skills, features, tools, and memory settings directly.

memoryTypeDialog.description: Choose how this agent should store and recall memory before submitting it.
